title: Heun RK2 matches the quadratic Taylor polynomial on the scalar linear test equation

source.citation: Ernst Hairer, Syvert P. Norsett, and Gerhard Wanner, Solving Ordinary Differential Equations I: Nonstiff Problems, 2nd ed., Springer, 1993, Ch. II, pp. 129-353.
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: For the scalar linear test equation y prime equals a y, one Heun RK2 step equals the second-order Taylor polynomial (1 + dt * a + (dt * a)^2 / 2) times y.
informal.latex: For \(y\prime=a y\), Heun RK2 gives \(y_+=(1+\Delta t\,a+(\Delta t\,a)^2/2)y\).
assumptions: dt, a, and y are real numbers
heunRK2Step averages the initial and endpoint slopes
linearTaylor2Step is the quadratic Taylor polynomial for the linear test equation
informal.expected_conclusion: The Heun RK2 step equals the linear-test-equation quadratic Taylor step.

declarations.name: heunRK2Step
declarations.statement: def heunRK2Step (dt a y : Real) : Real := let k1 := a * y; let k2 := a * (y + dt * k1); y + dt * ((k1 + k2) / 2)

declarations.name: linearTaylor2Step
declarations.statement: def linearTaylor2Step (dt a y : Real) : Real := (1 + dt * a + (dt * a) ^ 2 / 2) * y

declarations.name: heunRK2Step_eq_taylor2
declarations.statement: theorem heunRK2Step_eq_taylor2 (dt a y : Real) : heunRK2Step dt a y = linearTaylor2Step dt a y

tasks.input: Translate the Heun RK2 second-order Taylor identity for the scalar linear test equation into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem heunRK2Step_eq_taylor2 (dt a y : Real) : heunRK2Step dt a y = linearTaylor2Step dt a y

tasks.input: Given the heunRK2Step and linearTaylor2Step definitions, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof by unfolding heunRK2Step and linearTaylor2Step, then using ring.
